source: Bluetooth Core Specification, Vol 3, Part A (L2CAP Signaling)

0x0000.name: Connection successful
0x0000.description: The L2CAP channel was established successfully.

0x0001.name: Connection pending
0x0001.description: The connection is still being set up; a further response will follow (often pending authorization or authentication).
common_causes: Channel creation waiting on user authorization or an ongoing pairing procedure

0x0002.name: Connection refused - PSM not supported
0x0002.description: The remote device does not support/register the requested Protocol/Service Multiplexer (PSM), so it cannot route the channel to any service.
common_causes: Client tried to connect to a profile (e.g. A2DP/SDP/RFCOMM) the peer device doesn't implement or hasn't enabled
Wrong PSM value used by the client stack

0x0003.name: Connection refused - security block
0x0003.description: The remote device refused the channel because the link's current security level (authentication/encryption) doesn't meet the service's requirements.
common_causes: Profile requires an encrypted/authenticated link but pairing hasn't completed yet
Bonding was lost or keys are stale on one side

0x0004.name: Connection refused - no resources available
0x0004.description: The remote device lacks the resources (memory, channel table slots) to accept a new L2CAP channel.
common_causes: Peer already has many open channels/connections

0x0005.name: Connection refused - Invalid Source CID
0x0005.description: The source channel identifier in the request was invalid or already in use.
common_causes: Stack bug reusing a CID that's still allocated

0x0006.name: Connection refused - Source CID already allocated
0x0006.description: The requested source CID is already allocated to another channel.
common_causes: CID allocation collision, often from rapid reconnect without full teardown

0x0007.name: Connection refused - Unacceptable Parameters
0x0007.description: (LE Credit Based Flow Control) The proposed channel parameters (MTU, MPS, credits) were unacceptable.
common_causes: Requested LE L2CAP COC parameters exceed what the peer supports

0x0000.name: Command not understood
0x0000.description: The peer's L2CAP layer didn't recognize the signaling command code.
common_causes: Sender used a signaling command the peer's L2CAP version doesn't implement

0x0001.name: Signaling MTU exceeded
0x0001.description: The signaling packet exceeded the peer's signaling MTU.
common_causes: Oversized signaling PDU, often from a malformed or buggy stack

0x0002.name: Invalid CID in request
0x0002.description: The request referenced a channel identifier that doesn't exist.
common_causes: Stale CID used after the channel was already disconnected

0x0001.name: Failure - unacceptable parameters
0x0001.description: The proposed configuration parameters (e.g. MTU, flush timeout) were not acceptable.
common_causes: Requested MTU/flow-control parameters outside what the peer supports

0x0002.name: Failure - rejected (no reason)
0x0002.description: Configuration was rejected without a specific reason given.
common_causes: Generic peer-side rejection, often a vendor stack quirk

0x0003.name: Failure - unknown options
0x0003.description: The configuration request contained option types the peer doesn't recognize.
common_causes: Sender used an L2CAP configuration option newer than the peer's spec version

0x0004.name: Pending
0x0004.description: Configuration negotiation is still in progress.

0x0005.name: Failure - flow spec rejected
0x0005.description: The proposed flow-control specification was rejected.
common_causes: Requested QoS/flow parameters the peer cannot guarantee
